header_summary:
The wallet software now supports transaction replacements through Replace-By-Fee (RBF) mechanisms, even if the signaling bit is not present, enhancing usability across different devices without compromising security. This feature is particularly beneficial for legacy wallet transactions that do not support BIP125 signaling, providing a balance between user-friendliness and the technical complexities of blockchain management. More details on this implementation can be found [here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/178162039498.7.16213177200751410070.1421232845@slmail.me/T/#mf1437c3fbbe517e3e2976bec1440f07c45312cc7).

DigiMancer3D's development of a program to create unspendable dead Bitcoin addresses has sparked debates regarding the ethical and economic impacts of permanently removing bitcoins from circulation. This tool could potentially increase the scarcity and value of remaining bitcoins, or serve as a safeguard against accidental transactions, raising significant questions about the control and fate of digital assets within the cryptocurrency community. Further insights into this discussion are available [here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/pCw9QdcCGXxUzc0gbWCYvM1hEWe1YhGh0_GZR4J81dqItuq5nZ1S_MxrYh5G8sPi6UIcUXKcAh3-n9CyO_vC-gMFkEv-CpI_BoDowCQD_tA=@proton.me/T/#md15842d10f9e4cbe70242162efda443359a6c76e).

A significant vulnerability in LND versions prior to v0.20.1, related to handling zero-timestamp messages, has been addressed with new guidelines and updates to prevent potential denial of service attacks on the Lightning Network. This emphasizes the importance of continuous testing and swift response to maintain network security and stability. The detailed explanation and guidelines for this update can be accessed at [v0.20.1](https://github.com/lightningnetwork/lnd/releases/tag/v0.20.1-beta) and the related [pull request](https://github.com/lightningnetwork/lnd/pull/10469/files).

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Removal of BIP 125 RBF signalling in wallet transactions
recent_posts.summary:
- The wallet software permits transaction replacements via RBF, with associated user warnings.
- It supports legacy wallets not using BIP125 signaling, prioritizing safety and simplicity.
- The design targets secure, knowledgeable handling of blockchain transaction complexities.

recent_posts.title: Simple Setup for SPHINCS+ Bitcoin-style Address (proof-of-concept)
recent_posts.summary:
- A new program generates dead Bitcoin addresses that block fund retrieval.
- This initiative might intend to permanently reduce Bitcoin circulation, raising its value.
- The effort by DigiMancer3D might explore technical or ethical cryptocurrency themes.

recent_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy enhancements focus on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.
- CISA may lower costs but doesn't significantly boost privacy; the Lightning Network gains traction.
- Bitcoin's privacy strategies prioritize regulatory compatibility and minimal core disruptions.

recent_posts.title: LND: Zero-Timestamp Gossip DoS disclosure
recent_posts.summary:
- A critical vulnerability was found in LND versions before 0.20.1, necessitating urgent updates.
- LND v0.20.1 implements safety checks, blocking problematic zero-timestamp messages.
- Attackers could exploit this flaw via real or fake Lightning channels.

recent_posts.title: Towards A K-of-N Lightning Network Node
recent_posts.summary: - Shachains are calculated by \((n!)/(n-k+1)!(k-1)!\) for cryptographic security.  - Multiple holders of root secrets prevent single-point security failure.  - MuSig2 mitigates key cancellation risks in cryptographic transactions.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a rule to counteract SPV malleability in Bitcoin transactions.
- It targets specific 64-byte transactions that resemble Merkle node preimages.
- A minimal C++ function will check transactions to ensure block validity.

active_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts.summary: - The proposal modifies BIP360 to mandate a merkle path in P2MR control blocks.  - It aims to prohibit simple script trees in P2MR, enhancing security and privacy.  - Feedback is being collected to gauge the impact on privacy and fungibility.

active_posts.title: Proposal discussion: BIP39 native-language display wordlists
active_posts.summary: - The proposal enhances Bitcoin wallet recovery by introducing native language support.  - It aims to reduce user errors without changing the existing cryptographic process.  - Feedback is sought on its implementation and multilingual support via a GitHub repository.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal optimizes BIP-360 by enabling public key recovery from EC signatures.
- It enhances efficiency by reducing witness size without increasing quantum risk.
- Modifications to validation logic and nonce derivation are necessary for security.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- The Bitcoin-native LLM aims to enhance general LLMs in handling Bitcoin-specific protocols.
- It requires an extensive dataset from diverse Bitcoin-centric resources for thorough training.
- The LLM's architecture includes offline analysis and live data querying capabilities for varied use.

active_posts.title: Binary Fuse filters as an alternative to BIP 158 GCS
active_posts.summary:
- The event explored set-membership algorithms for Bitcoin light clients, evaluating Binary Fuse filters.
- Preliminary tests showed the Fuse16 filter variant significantly reduced CPU usage while minimally increasing bandwidth.
- Further benchmarking development is considered to enhance framework modularity and community usability.

today_in_history_posts.title: Standardisation of an unstructured taproot annex
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Joost highlighted annex feature benefits for Bitcoin's time-locked vaults' safety and accessibility.
- He developed a demo showing the annex's role in securing pre-signed transactions.
- Raising awareness about annex-enabled on-chain signature backups is crucial for implementation.

today_in_history_posts.title: Potential vulnerability in Lightning backends: BOLT-11 "payment hash" does not commit to payment!
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- LNbits patched an exploit in version 0.10.5; users should update immediately.
- The exploit involved creating fraudulent balances with manipulated payment hashes.
- Developers are advised to enhance security against similar vulnerabilities in Lightning applications.

today_in_history_posts.title: Estimating Likelihood for Lightning Payments to be (in)feasible
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rene Pickhardt is developing a paper on mathematical theories for payment networks.
- His research addresses inaccuracies in liquidity modeling within the Lightning Network.
- Pickhardt’s new method calculates payment success without knowing exact liquidity.
